target_name: Linus Torvalds — Correctness Audit (All 3 Projects, Post-Fix Verification)
target_cmd: python3 -c "import subprocess, sys; results = []; cmds = [('solace-browser', 'python3 -m pytest /home/phuc/projects/solace-browser/tests/ -q --tb=line -k evidence or compliance or vault or pipeline --no-header 2>&1 | tail -3'), ('solaceagi', 'python3 -m pytest /home/phuc/projects/solaceagi/tests/app_v1/ -q --tb=line --ignore=tests/app_v1/__pycache__ --ignore=tests/app_v1/test_task_001_auth.py --no-header 2>&1 | tail -3'), ('solace-cli', 'cd /home/phuc/projects/solace-cli && python3 -m pytest tests/ -q --tb=line --ignore=tests/test_todo_commands.py --no-header 2>&1 | tail -3')]; [results.append(f'{name}: {subprocess.getoutput(cmd)}') for name, cmd in cmds]; print('\n'.join(results))"

checks.llm_verdict_required: Verify all 3 projects pass tests post Linus fixes. Report any failures.

domain_questions: Does _DefaultPzip.compress() actually compress data (zlib, not hash)?
Does the vault key persist across process restarts?
Are all compliance checks actually validating (not hardcoded passed=True)?
Do HTTP error responses return non-200 status codes?
Is credit deduction happening after LLM calls?
Is the PBKDF2 salt no longer hardcoded to b'solaceagi'?
Is the Firestore singleton not stuck-broken on init failure?
Is the cron secret compared constant-time?
Are vault directory permissions set to 0o700?
context: Post-fix verification: Linus Torvalds found 20 real bugs across 3 projects. All were fixed in GLOW 116 (commits: ea63fd1, 7cb06fd, a979b4d). This spec verifies the fixes hold and no regressions were introduced.
